samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Converts parallel data to serial format.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal for serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="cnt" width_description="[2:0]">Counter for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="valid" width_description="[2:0]">Valid signal for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data" width_description="[3:0]">Data register for parallel data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module parallel2serial converts parallel data to serial format. 
    The module has 4-bit parallel data input (d) and a serial data output (dout). 
    The module uses a counter (cnt) to control the serial output. 
    When the counter is 3, the module outputs the most significant bit of the parallel data. 
    The counter is then reset to 0 and the valid signal is set to 1. 
    The remaining bits of the parallel data are then shifted to the left. 
    The counter is incremented by 1 and the valid signal is set to 0. 
    This process is repeated until all bits of the parallel data are converted to serial format.
    </implementation>
</module>
